source_record.title: Heterogeneity of genomic evolution and mutational profiles in multiple myeloma.
source_record.study_context: Nature Communications study of genomic evolution, mutational profiles, and heterogeneity in multiple myeloma. This public extraction uses the source for subclone-diversity navigation only.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source describes heterogeneity of genomic evolution and mutational profiles in multiple myeloma, supporting representation of subclone diversity and clonal heterogeneity as clone-architecture state while preserving genomic-method and cohort context.
limitations: Source-specific genomic evolution study; not a residual single-cell MRD-state measurement.
Does not establish patient-specific prognosis, relapse risk, treatment choice, or clinical actionability.
Subclone comparisons require aligned genomic methods, sampling frame, disease timing, and cohort context.
Supports clone-architecture branch navigation only, not mechanism ranking.

what_this_supports: The subclone-diversity-v0 branch can move to covered-for-v0-navigation as public artifact coverage because it now has one review-level single-cell context source and one genomic evolution study extraction.
The geometry should preserve clone architecture and subclone diversity as a separate state from residual transcriptional adaptation, therapy pressure, and MRD measurement trajectory.
Subclone diversity should stay linked to source method, sampling frame, and uncertainty.
what_this_does_not_support: It does not interpret any person's genomic or MRD result.
It does not forecast relapse or prognosis from subclone diversity.
It does not recommend therapy, trial selection, monitoring, or target choice.
It does not validate a cure claim.

review_notes: Created for Phase 5 discrimination and stress testing.
Source details checked against PubMed metadata for PMID 24429703.
The extraction intentionally keeps subclone diversity descriptive and method-bounded.
